You are a terminal assistant. Translate user requests into bash commands. If a request is ambiguous or potentially dangerous, explain why instead of running it. If the request is a question that doesn't need a command, answer it directly without calling any tools.
Write a bash script that accepts a -n flag for dry-run and -o for output file using getopts

<think> getopts with n flag (no arg) and o: flag (requires arg). </think> <tool_call><function=run_bash><parameter=command>cat << 'EOF' > deploy.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
DRY_RUN=0
OUTPUT=""
while getopts "no:" opt; do
  case $opt in
    n) DRY_RUN=1 ;;
    o) OUTPUT="$OPTARG" ;;
    *) echo "Usage: $0 [-n] [-o outfile]" >&2; exit 1 ;;
  esac
done
shift $((OPTIND - 1))
[[ $DRY_RUN -eq 1 ]] && echo "[DRY RUN] No changes will be made"
[[ -n $OUTPUT ]] && echo "Output: $OUTPUT"
EOF
chmod +x deploy.sh</parameter></function></tool_call>

Write a bash script that retries a command up to 3 times with a 5-second delay between attempts

<think> Loop with counter, break on success, sleep between failures. </think> <tool_call><function=run_bash><parameter=command>cat << 'EOF' > retry.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
retry() {
  local attempts=3
  local delay=5
  local cmd=("$@")
  for ((i=1; i<=attempts; i++)); do
    "${cmd[@]}" && return 0
    echo "Attempt $i/$attempts failed. Retrying in ${delay}s..." >&2
    sleep "$delay"
  done
  echo "All $attempts attempts failed." >&2
  return 1
}
retry curl -f https://example.com/api/health

Create a systemd timer that runs /usr/local/bin/cleanup.sh every hour

<think>
Systemd timers require two unit files: a .service and a .timer. We create both and enable the timer.
</think>

<tool_call>
<function=run_bash>
<parameter=command>
sudo bash -c 'cat > /etc/systemd/system/cleanup.service << EOF
[Unit]
Description=Cleanup Script

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/cleanup.sh
EOF

cat > /etc/systemd/system/cleanup.timer << EOF
[Unit]
Description=Run cleanup every hour

[Timer]
OnCalendar=hourly
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
EOF

systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl enable --now cleanup.timer'
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
